intro: Chabraea tomentosa, also known as Kalmegh, is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and inflammation, due to its potential to modulate the immune system and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Chabraea tomentosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chabraea tomentosa is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or extract, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as anorexia, coughs, and respiratory issues.
